channel.title: china-static:tech
channel.description: Static RSS merge for china variant (tech)

item.title: Walmart-backed Flipkart expands quick-commerce push as Amazon ramps up in India
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Walmart-backed Flipkart has crossed 1,000 micro-fulfillment centers as Amazon accelerates its own quick-commerce push in India.

item.title: India’s MoEngage bets that the future of marketing is millions of AI agents
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: The all-cash deal gives MoEngage access to technology that assigns AI agents to individual customers.

item.title: Superhuman acquires AI detection startup GPTZero
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Superhuman, which also has an AI detection tool as part of Grammarly, has snapped up GPTZero.

item.title: After betting the firm on Anthropic, Menlo Ventures raises victorious $3B fund
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Menlo has created a solid rep for itself as an AI investor, all based on one gutsy $750 million move in 2024.

item.title: Klue says hackers stole credential from 2022 that led to customer data breaches
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: It's unclear why Klue had not revoked the credential after the limited pilot, which hackers then used to breach a system holding keys for accessing customers' data.

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg wants Meta to launch its own prediction market
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: The app would be independent of Meta's other social media offerings, although sources told the NYT that those social sites could direct users to engagement with the app.

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude Tag is learning your company, one Slack message at a time
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Anthropic’s new Claude Tag brings an always-on AI teammate to Slack. But beyond productivity, the feature is a strategic play to capture organizational context, institutional knowledge, and enterprise workflows.

item.title: Password manager maker LastPass says hackers stole customer support case data during Klue breach
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: This is the second data breach to affect LastPass customers in recent years, after one of the password manager's tech partners was recently breached.

item.title: HaloBraid raises $7M from Seven Seven Six to end the six-hour hair salon appointment
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: HaloBraid aims to help salons speed up braiding with its first device, slated to launch later this year, that acts as a braiding assistant for professional stylists.

item.title: Meta debuts new, cheaper smart glasses under its own brand
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: The smart glasses are available in several countries starting today in a variety of color and lens combinations.

item.title: The best robot vacuum deals available during Prime Day
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: If you’ve been wanting to buy a robot vacuum but have been put off by how much it can cost to get a good one, now is not a bad time to start looking. Prime Day has kicked off, though more than just Amazon is offering deals on several models we’ve tested from brands like […]

item.title: This year’s Prime Day deals on Apple products are the best I’ve seen
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Amazon’s Prime Day sale is here, and whether you’re looking for a new pair of wireless earbuds or a smartwatch, there’s a good chance you’ll find a discount. The Apple Watch Series 11 has already dropped to a new low price, while the AirPods Pro 3 recently hit a record-low $169 at Walmart. That particular […]

item.title: The Nex Playground is down to its pre-RAMageddon price during Prime Day
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: The Nex Playground is the family-centric, Kinect-like game console that made one Verge editor’s kids laugh, cry, and ask for more playtime, even when they were sick. The motion-based game play isn’t perfect, but it has won over plenty of parents — and it’s on sale for $239 at Amazon, down from its current MSRP […]

item.title: Google Home will soon get better at recognizing you
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: A new update for Google Home could make it less likely your smart home cameras mistake you for someone else, just because you're facing away from the camera. Starting June 23rd, Google's expanding its facial recognition feature so that people you've tagged in your Familiar Faces library can continue to be identified when their faces […]

item.title: Hollywood is bending the knee to OpenAI
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Netflix, A24, Focus Features, and Warner Bros.' Clockwork have all reportedly decided to pass on picking up Artificial - director Luca Guadagnino's new biographical drama about OpenAI cofounder / CEO Sam Altman - for distribution deals. And while Neon and Mubi are still said to be interested in the film, this situation makes it seem […]

item.title: Prime Day takes $240 off Roborock’s Saros 20, one of our favorite robovacs
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: The best robot vacuums are the ones you barely have to think about, and the Roborock Saros 20 fits that description well. It’s why it’s one of our favorite robovac / mop hybrids, and thanks to Prime Day, you can get it on sale at Amazon and Roborock for $1,359.99 ($240 off), a new low […]

item.title: The Meta Quest 3S is on sale for $297 — which is basically its old price
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: The Meta Quest 3S VR headset with 128GB of storage is $296.79 (about $53 off) at Amazon. This is Meta’s entry-level VR headset, which launched back in 2024 for $299.99 before getting a price increase this year to $349.99. So it’s not exactly the most exciting deal, but it’s about as good as you’re likely […]

item.title: The best Prime Day deals we found on our favorite gear
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Amazon’s Prime Day is on, and it’s happening for the next four days. Prime members can jump into the deals now until the sale officially ends at 3:01AM ET / 12:01AM PT June 27th. Many discounts will remain the same throughout the duration. We’ve been covering Prime Day in-depth since the shopping event debuted over […]

item.title: Xteink’s tiny e-readers are 20 percent off for Prime Day
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: The Xteink X4 and smaller X3 e-readers are discounted to $55.20 (regularly $69) and $63.20 (normally $79), respectively, as part of Amazon’s Prime Day promotions this week. Both e-readers are significantly smaller and more pocketable than any of the Kindle and Kobo models currently available, and feature magnetic mounts so they can be stuck to […]

item.title: Prime Day has some solid deals on Switch 2, PS5, and Xbox games
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: There are some some sizable discounts on new and recent hits from the last few years for the Nintendo Switch 2, PlayStation 5, and the Xbox Series X / S for Prime Day. Most Amazon game deals are physical copies, but every so often there’s a better or equivalent price on digital versions, as well […]

item.title: White House drastically shortens deadline for dropping quantum-vulnerable crypto
item.dc:creator: Ars Technica
item.description: Order warns of national security risks if post-quantum cryptography isn't adopted in time.

item.title: Oracle’s 21,000 layoffs help drive its debt-fueled AI investments
item.dc:creator: Ars Technica
item.description: Oracle is spending billions on data center infrastructure to support AI.

item.title: Following user outcry, AMD reinstates memory encryption in consumer CPUs
item.dc:creator: Ars Technica
item.description: Critics saw the move as an underhanded way to steer them toward more costly chips.

item.title: Microsoft discovers new lightweight backdoor that steals cryptocurrency
item.dc:creator: Ars Technica
item.description: Crypto Clipper spreads over USB and communicates over Tor.

item.title: Apple patches high-severity eavesdropping vulnerability in Beats Studio Buds
item.dc:creator: Ars Technica
item.description: The vulnerability, disclosed 12 months ago, affects multiple manufacturers.

item.title: Before SpaceX IPO, investors in China secretly acquired stakes
item.dc:creator: Ars Technica
item.description: One previously unreported SpaceX investor has ties to Chinese military contractors.

item.title: Massive breach spills credentials for thousands of sensitive networks
item.dc:creator: Ars Technica
item.description: The affected include Oracle, Lenovo, FedEx, a NATO contractor, and Fortinet.

item.title: Tesco moving 40,000 server workloads off VMware amid Broadcom's “abusive conduct”
item.dc:creator: Ars Technica
item.description: Tesco claimed Broadcom hiked its VMware prices by about 175 percent in UK court filings.

item.title: "Dangerous" AI models are coming no matter what
item.dc:creator: Ars Technica
item.description: AI models with advanced hacking capabilities will soon be the norm.

item.title: Windows and Linux users: The deadline to update Secure Boot keys is near
item.dc:creator: Ars Technica
item.description: What you need to know about the expiration of keys securing your machine's boot sequence.
